skill.name: frontend-components
skill.description: Guides agents to build reusable React UI components with focused responsibilities, clear props, composition patterns, and practical state management.
skill.summary: Build maintainable React components with clear APIs and focused responsibilities.
skill.author: DevanB

security_audit.summary: The static external command and weak cryptography findings are false positives caused by Markdown text, not executable code. A real filesystem concern remains because SKILL.md references a parent-directory path outside the packaged skill content. No prompt injection, network access, credential access, or confirmed malicious behavior was found.

medium_findings.title: Out-of-Package Relative File Reference
medium_findings.description: SKILL.md links to ../../../agent-os/standards/frontend/components.md. This is not confirmed malicious, but it directs the agent to content outside the packaged skill boundary.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The parent-directory traversal sequence is explicit on line 28. The context appears to be a documentation reference, so the risk is boundary exposure rather than confirmed exfiltration.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Inline Paths Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Lines 12 and 13 use backticks for Markdown inline formatting around file extensions and a component directory path. No Ruby code, shell command, or command execution instruction appears there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The detected backticks are Markdown formatting in a guidance document. There is no executable file, interpreter context, or dynamic command input.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts on lines 3, 8, and 14 occur in descriptive prose. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing API, or security-sensitive transformation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines contain natural-language skill metadata and frontend guidance. I found no evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, or similar weak cryptographic use.

dangerous_patterns.title: Parent Directory Traversal in Skill Reference
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill uses a ../../../ relative path in a Markdown link. This can cause agents to consult files outside the packaged skill if those files exist in the surrounding workspace.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The path traversal sequence is directly visible on line 28. The surrounding wording suggests a standards-file reference, not a deliberate attempt to read secrets.

content.value_statement: Frontend teams need components that stay small, reusable, and easy to change. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code toward clear React component APIs and maintainable composition.
actual_capabilities: Guides creation of React components in .tsx and .jsx files.
Supports clear prop interfaces and TypeScript component types.
Encourages composition with smaller focused components.
Helps decide when to use local state or lift state upward.
Applies reusable component patterns for buttons, cards, modals, and forms.
Supports refactoring large components into smaller units.
limitations: The packaged skill only includes SKILL.md and references an external standards file.
It provides guidance, not a complete component library.
It does not define automated tests or lint rules.
It assumes a React-style frontend project.

use_cases.title: Create Shared UI Components
use_cases.description: Build reusable components with focused responsibilities, typed props, and predictable state boundaries.

use_cases.title: Refactor Large React Files
use_cases.description: Split large components into smaller units while preserving behavior and simplifying future maintenance.

use_cases.title: Standardize Component APIs
use_cases.description: Review component interfaces for clear names, minimal props, and practical composition patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Component
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a reusable React component for this UI need. Keep the props clear, state minimal, and responsibilities focused.

prompt_templates.title: Review Component Props
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this component interface. Suggest clearer prop names, better TypeScript types, and any props that should be removed or split.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor Component Structure
prompt_templates.prompt: Refactor this large React component into smaller components. Preserve behavior and explain the new component responsibilities.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Component System
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a small set of reusable components for this workflow. Include composition rules, state ownership, and documentation notes.

output_examples.input: A developer asks for a reusable modal component.
output_examples.output: The response defines the modal responsibilities, required props, optional callbacks, accessibility expectations, and local state boundaries.

output_examples.input: A maintainer provides a large form component.
output_examples.output: The response proposes smaller field, section, validation, and action components with clear ownership for each part.

output_examples.input: A team wants consistent shadcn/ui customization.
output_examples.output: The response describes component wrapper rules, prop naming standards, and where shared variants should live.

best_practices: Keep each component focused on one clear responsibility.
Prefer explicit prop interfaces over implicit data dependencies.
Document reusable components with practical usage examples.
anti_patterns: Do not combine unrelated UI behavior in one component.
Do not expose internal state details as public component API.
Do not create shared components before reuse needs are clear.

faq.question: What frontend framework does this skill target?
faq.answer: It targets React components, especially .tsx and .jsx files.

faq.question: Can it help with TypeScript props?
faq.answer: Yes. It gives guidance for prop interfaces, TypeScript types, and clearer component contracts.

faq.question: Does it include ready-made components?
faq.answer: No. It provides component design guidance rather than a shipped UI library.

faq.question: Can it support shadcn/ui work?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill explicitly covers building and customizing shadcn/ui components.

faq.question: Is this safe for marketplace publication?
faq.answer: It has a medium warning because it references a file outside the packaged skill. No malicious behavior was confirmed.

faq.question: What should publishers fix before release?
faq.answer: They should package the referenced standards file or replace the parent-directory link with local guidance.
